title: Managing rental inventory?
excerpt: Anyone managing rental inventory on Monday? Tracking gear in/out and availability?

posts.author: jpelrman87 (Josh Perlman)
posts.cookedHtml: Anyone managing rental inventory on Monday? Tracking gear in/out and availability?

posts.author: TinyFoxLabs (TinyFox Labs)
posts.cookedHtml: I do not manage rental inventory on Monday, but I check and there is a template for that. You can try it (+ => Board => Start with template => search for “Rental Property Spreadsheet”) to see if it meets/satisfy your needs. If you have more questions tag me in this post or write me a personal message.

posts.author: jpelrman87 (Josh Perlman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks- that is for managing rental proprieties like houses and apartments.

We are looking for managing inventory that goes out on a project then comes back

posts.author: Johnsa9 (Ashley Morrison)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have an inventory board I use to track our uniforms. I am sure there is a better way to do it but it’s at least a starting point. I use “The Monday Man” column magic and better math automations.

posts.author: tio.escarda (Tio Escarda)
posts.cookedHtml: We manage our equipment/gear inventory on monday. I work with a cabinet shop, so we need to track a lot of equipment and tooling locations and status. We actually developed an app called Q-ARGH which allows us to generate branded, custom labels with QR codes for all our equipment so the shop floor folks and maintenance/safety manager can interact directly with the assets using a mobile device or PC. Check us out on the marketplace for a free trial, or shoot me a PM if you want to bat any ideas around.

posts.author: andreatew (Andrea Tew)
posts.cookedHtml: Following! We have started a board for tracking arlo cameras, but it could definitely use some improvement!

posts.author: johannesgeyer (Johannes Geyer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jauerbach123 , Hi @jpelrman87 ,

You can use the DocExport PDF Generator app to create QR code labels for your items, which can then be printed and attached to shelves or rental items.

When scanned, the QR codes allow users to input the current stock count, automatically updating the numbers column on your monday board. This ensures inventory levels remain accurate without requiring direct access to the board or a monday user account/login. It supports status columns and numbers columns.

Here’s a quick video tutorial of how to set up the “Triggers” feature with DocExport:

posts.author: Avo_FluidApps (Avo)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @jpelrman87 - you might find QR Item Tracker helpful! It lets you scan gear in and out, track availability, and log everything right inside monday.com, super handy for rental workflows.
